title: SSL error when using ssl in an amd64 container running on arm64 macbook
body:
### Issue Description

I am getting ssl certificate errors when running x86 images on my m2 macbook.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -it --platform=linux/amd64 --rm python:3-alpine /usr/bin/wget https://podman.io/


### Describe the results you received

When using `--platform=linux/amd64` the ssl cert verify fails.

```
$ podman run -it --platform=linux/amd64 --rm python:3-alpine /usr/bin/wget https://podman.io/
Connecting to podman.io (185.199.108.153:443)
48FB8D0240000000:error:0200008A:rsa routines:RSA_padding_check_PKCS1_type_1:invalid padding:crypto/rsa/rsa_pk1.c:75:
48FB8D0240000000:error:02000072:rsa routines:rsa_ossl_public_decrypt:padding check failed:crypto/rsa/rsa_ossl.c:606:
48FB8D0240000000:error:1C880004:Provider routines:rsa_verify:RSA lib:providers/implementations/signature/rsa_sig.c:774:
48FB8D0240000000:error:06880006:asn1 encoding routines:ASN1_item_verify_ctx:EVP lib:crypto/asn1/a_verify.c:217:
48FB8D0240000000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1889:
ssl_client: SSL_connect
wget: error getting response: Connection reset by peer

```

With the arm64 image it works fine:

```
$ podman run -it --platform=linux/arm64 --rm python:3-alpine /usr/bin/wget https://podman.io/
Resolved "python" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/python:3-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:9e95ea84eb4890ec274983dda85d7b32e41adbd283ad61c3dd895fcbf158a3f3
Copying blob sha256:5fd9bafbcd0902041a3f44c51f472061aa4e93d8755f80f25d200f9b3a597763
Copying blob sha256:74b3a271aef90b25de0dfddcaf22c79ffa1fc32f1e9eac89f21fdd8162c30666
Copying blob sha256:c41833b44d910632b415cd89a9cdaa4d62c9725dc56c99a7ddadafd6719960f9
Copying blob sha256:6c89684ac2a52710830ffb671582de220d030b83718680da6a7c6d3408dbf769
Copying config sha256:1ee10431f9b87c927f5410ba39462129e4a9b7b21a574eed2bcc27e5dcf8eea9
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Connecting to podman.io (185.199.108.153:443)
saving to 'index.html'
index.html           100% |****************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************| 83643  0:00:00 ETA
'index.html' saved
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected wget to succesfully retrieve index.html.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Macbook Pro M2

`Darwin lappie-returns.local 22.4.0 Darwin Kernel Version 22.4.0: Mon Mar  6 20:59:58 PST 2023; root:xnu-8796.101.5~3/RELEASE_ARM64_T6020 arm64`

### Additional information

I updated to 4.5.0 a day ago and I'm not sure if I've actually successfully used ssl in a container since the update.

comments.body: I was able to resolve my issue by updating the podman machine image (setting `--image-path stable` explicitly).
